observations: REV2 DELTA -- both evaluator rev1 false-negatives CLOSED at the design level, using the evaluator's OWN recommended fix direction (issues[0].required_fix option (i): drop the cap; issues[1].required_fix: stop treating ;/: as terminators). Sub-check (1)'s join is now CAP-FREE and SENTENCE-TERMINAL-ONLY: a group closes ONLY when the appended physical line ends in `.`/`!`/`?` (optionally followed by a closing quote/paren/emphasis char and trailing whitespace) OR at a blank line; `;` and `:` no longer close a group, and there is no line-count cap. Each closed group -> the UNMODIFIED vlib_no_stale_payload exactly once. The evaluator had already independently pre-validated this exact 'sentence-terminal-only, no-cap' variant (rev1 qa issues[0] point (4)): it closes the rev0 false-pass equally well, catches both rev1 false-negative fixtures, and does not disturb the guide's ASCII <pre> directory-tree block.

REV2 DELTA -- traced the two NEW regression fixtures against the rev2 join and both behave as the contract claims: (a) tools/fixtures/stale_payload_longwrap.md ('...the following:' / `standard/`, / `templates/`, / and `schema/` only.) -- line 2 ends in `:` (no longer a terminator) and no cap fragments the 5-line span, so the whole sentence reconstructs to its real terminal period and vlib_no_stale_payload catches the missing tools/ -> [C10] FAIL payload-set. Directly closes rev1 issues[0] (the hard-cap fragmentation false-negative). (b) tools/fixtures/stale_payload_semicolon.md ('...`standard/`, `templates/`;' / `schema/` only.) -- line 1 ends in `;` (no longer a terminator), so the group is not force-closed at the semicolon and reconstructs across both physical lines -> [C10] FAIL payload-set. Directly closes rev1 issues[1] (the ;/: force-close false-negative). Each fixture is paired with a neutral-mktemp anti-cheat entry (verification[14], verification[16]) matching the existing methodology/02 Sec 10 anti-cheat convention.

REV2 DELTA -- GIP.md item-3 pre-existing defect independently re-traced under the rev2 no-cap join and is STILL genuinely caught (fix remains FORCED). standard/GIP.md lines 103-106: L103 ends 'or', L104 ends 'the', L105 ends 'are' (the '...local mutation.' period is MID-line, followed by 'Locally hand-patched...'), L106 ends 'well-intentioned.' (line-terminal period -> closes). The group is therefore lines 103-106, containing standard/+templates/+schema/ and NO tools/ -> vlib_no_stale_payload FAILS; adding tools/ to the enumeration makes the group name all four -> PASS. Outcome ('exactly one default-mode sub-check-1 failure without the fix, zero with it') is CORRECT and the non-breaking AT2 gate holds.

NON-BLOCKING (design_notes narration imprecision, refreshed class carried from rev0/rev1): pre_existing_finding_gip states the GIP.md item-3 group 'closes its own group strictly at its own terminal period -- the physical line "consumer has drifted through local mutation." ends in `.`'. That physical line (L105) does NOT end in a period -- it ends in 'are'; the actual line-terminal close is L106 ('...well-intentioned.'). The described CLOSE MECHANISM is inaccurate, but the OUTCOME (item 3 caught, fix forced, non-breaking gate holds) is correct and independently traced above. This is post-hoc rationale, not an Executor instruction (the close conditions themselves -- line-terminal `.`/`!`/`?` or blank line, no cap -- are stated correctly and unambiguously), and verification[23] asserts on actual validate.sh OUTPUT + the Section-4 span content, so it fails closed if mis-implemented. Recommend tightening the sentence to name L106's period; does not block approval.

NON-BLOCKING (regex micro-syntax double-check, carried from evaluator rev1 observation): design_notes offers the '`]`-first' escape-free bracket form for condition (a) ([.!?] followed by an optional run of closing chars `]`*'")  then trailing whitespace to line end), claiming it is gawk-warning-free per the standard POSIX convention of placing the literal `]` first. The structural technique (literal `]` first) is correct and the close-condition semantics are fully specified; the exact bracket-expression bytes the generator lands in tools/validate.sh's awk invocation should be double-checked against a real shell (not by visual inspection) to confirm no stray backslash-escape warning. Non-blocking: the content-driven fixtures (verification[3],[11],[13],[15]) and their neutral-mktemp anti-cheats fail closed if the regex mis-fires, so a malformed bracket cannot silently pass.

CARRIED (still holds under rev2) -- scope fidelity: scope.files_modify is exactly the 4 files (tools/validate.sh, docs/guide/index.html, methodology/06_release_train.md, standard/GIP.md); scope.files_create is 6 fixtures (stale_payload.md, stale_payload_cosentence.md, stale_payload_longwrap.md, stale_payload_semicolon.md, bad_discovery_order.md, version_drift.html), ALL under tools/fixtures/. The AT9 scope-guard allow-list (verification[8]: vlib_scope_guard over the 4 modify paths + the 'tools/fixtures/' prefix) already covers the two NEW fixtures with NO allow-list edit. Nothing else added.

CARRIED (still holds under rev2) -- compositional discipline: non_goals[2] keeps tools/verify_lib.sh untouched; the cap-free sentence-terminal join is PREPROCESSING inside check_c10_consistency composing the vetted, unmodified vlib_no_stale_payload exactly once per file. The cap-free variant is a MORE faithful realization of sub-check (1)'s stated purpose ('no stale enumeration survives ANYWHERE') than the rejected capped rev1, since it catches enumerations of arbitrary physical length.

CARRIED (still holds under rev2) -- all 10 feature_list_004 F-027 acceptance tests map 1:1 to verification[0..9]; the 15 ADDITIONAL entries (verification[10..24]) are additive soundness: anti-cheat neutral-filename copies, the rev0/rev1 regression fixtures (cosentence, longwrap, semicolon), the two payload/html 3-mode pinning proofs, and the NDEBT-003(a/b) + GIP remediation proofs. Verification count is 25 (0..24); evidence_convention updated to 027-verify-25.txt; estimated_lines 200 -- a reasonable additive delta for the 2 new fixtures + 4 new entries.

CARRIED (still holds under rev2) -- C8 per-file discipline (standard/GIP.md and methodology/06_release_train.md each bump 0.2.0->0.3.0 with one new change_log entry; docs/guide/index.html carries no frontmatter and no bump obligation), the four narrow content fixes (guide key-doc 05_eval_and_trace.md addition; 06_release_train.md bare-token 05->06; GIP.md item-3 tools/ addition), P3 discovery-order scoping (both canonical labels required, CONTEXT.md exempt by content), the External Anchor Rule (json.load(open('NIZAM.json'))['framework']['version']), the forced new *.html --target case, and 3-mode pinning (default 10/0, .md+.html targets, payload 9/0) are all unchanged from the approved rev0/rev1 and remain correct.

NON-BLOCKING (carried from rev0/rev1): feature_list_004 F-027 AT9's literal wording allow-lists only tools/validate.sh + tools/fixtures/, narrower than the contract's actually-necessary 5-file scope; the contract asserts the corrected 5-entry set via vlib_scope_guard (verification[8]), mirroring the already-approved F-026 AT8 in-place amendment. RECOMMENDATION for the orchestrator: after approval, amend feature_list_004 F-027 AT9 in-place to the 5-file set for durable-record consistency.

NON-BLOCKING (carried from rev0/rev1): spec P2's literal version numbers ('0.1.0 == 0.1.0') are stale pre-v0.5.x-rebase artifacts; on-disk reality is 0.5.1 == 0.5.1. Because sub-check (3) reads the LIVE NIZAM.json value per the External Anchor Rule, the stale spec literal does not bind the implementation. No action required.

final_verdict.summary: APPROVED (rev2). Both evaluator rev1 blockers are faithfully closed at the design level using the evaluator's own recommended fix directions: the join is now CAP-FREE (closing issues[0], the hard-3-line-cap fragmentation false-negative) and SENTENCE-TERMINAL-ONLY with `;`/`:` no longer terminators (closing issues[1], the semicolon/colon force-close false-negative) -- a group closes ONLY at a line-terminal `.`/`!`/`?` (optionally + a closing quote/paren/emphasis char) or a blank line, and each closed group is handed to the unmodified vlib_no_stale_payload once. The two required regression fixtures were added under tools/fixtures/ (stale_payload_longwrap.md for the >3-line unpunctuated wrap; stale_payload_semicolon.md for the internal-`;` break), each with a FAIL assertion and a neutral-mktemp anti-cheat entry; both were independently traced to fire [C10] FAIL payload-set as claimed. The GIP.md item-3 defect was independently re-traced under the no-cap join (group = lines 103-106, closing at L106's line-terminal period, containing standard/+templates/+schema/ without tools/) and is STILL genuinely caught -- the fix remains forced and AT2's 'SUMMARY: 10 passed, 0 failed' non-breaking gate holds. Scope fidelity (4 modify + 6 fixtures, all fixtures under the already-allow-listed tools/fixtures/), compositional discipline (verify_lib.sh untouched), the 1:1 mapping of all 10 acceptance tests to verification[0..9], C8 per-file version-bump discipline, the four narrow content fixes, P3 discovery-order scoping, the External Anchor Rule, the forced *.html --target case, and 3-mode pinning are all unchanged and correct; verification 21->25, evidence_convention 027-verify-25.txt, estimated_lines 200 are reasonable additive deltas. Zero blocking discrepancies. Non-blocking observations: (1) pre_existing_finding_gip's close-mechanism prose again mis-names the closing period (says L105 'local mutation.'; the actual line-terminal close is L106 'well-intentioned.') -- narration imprecision only, outcome correct and self-verified by verification[23]; (2) the generator should byte-check the escape-free `]`-first awk bracket expression against a real shell for gawk warnings (fixtures fail closed regardless); (3) carried recommendations to amend feature_list F-027 AT9 to the 5-file scope in-place and that the stale spec-P2 version literals are non-binding.
